issue_title: [ENG-003] Implement canonical GameState model + JSON-serializable invariants baseline
rendered_issue_body:
<!-- Generated by tools/spec_board_sync.ts from the approved story file. -->

_This issue is a synchronized projection of the approved story. Edit the story file, then rerun the sync tool instead of hand-editing authoritative sections here._

## Summary
Implement the canonical GameState data model requirements (server-only, serializable arrays, branded IDs) and run required invariants after every accepted action and decision resume in tests/dev.

**Story ID:** `ENG-003`  
**Spec Version:** `v6`  
**Type:** `implementation`  
**Area:** `engine`  
**Priority:** `critical`  
**Status:** `approved`

## Authoritative Spec References
- 00-project-overview.s010 (State is serializable)
- 03-game-state-events-decisions.s002 (Canonical state model)
- 03-game-state-events-decisions.s007 (Atomic mutation contract)
- 03-game-state-events-decisions.s021 (Invariant hooks)
- 03-game-state-events-decisions.s022 (Internal state sequencing)
- 18-acceptance-tests.s009 (Global invariants)

## Scope
- Define/align engine-core canonical GameState shape with required fields (seq/stateSeq, actionSeq, turn, players, battle?, pendingDecision?, effectQueue, deferredTriggers, continuousEffects, replacementState, revealedCards, rng, eventJournal, audit).
- Ensure canonical state uses JSON-serializable structures (no JS Set/Map in state; arrays for deterministic hashing inputs).
- Implement invariant hook runner callable by engine loop after each accepted action and decision resume in dev/test mode.
- Implement each required invariant listed in spec as separate functions with structured failure details.

## Out of Scope
- Network envelopes or match-server protocol handling.
- Effect DSL runtime execution beyond validating queue entries have valid sources/policies (no new effect semantics here).
- Database persistence or replay artifacts.

## Dependencies
- ENG-001 (#7)
- CON-001 (#3)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GameState, actions, events, decisions remain JSON-serializable (no functions, Dates, Sets).
- [ ] All invariants in 03-game-state-events-decisions.s021 are executed after every accepted action and after every decision resume in dev/test mode.
- [ ] Invariant failures produce EngineError {type:'invariantViolation'}-compatible details or throw in tests (implementation choice) without mutating state further.
- [ ] G-001..G-012 invariants from 18-acceptance-tests.s009 pass for baseline flows used in Milestone 1 acceptance tests.

## Required Tests
- Unit: each invariant function fails on a minimal constructed counterexample and passes on a minimal valid state.
- Integration: engine applyAction/resumeDecision invokes invariant runner in dev/test mode (spy/assert call count).
- Property/serialization: JSON.stringify(GameState) roundtrip does not drop required fields (except brands) and remains stable.

## Repo Rules
- Do not add non-serializable objects into canonical state.
- Prefer pure functions; do not mutate state in place in production logic per atomic mutation contract.

## Ambiguity Policy
implement_if_clearly_implied
